name: Pipeline Agents
description: 19 agent definitions forming the multi-stage pipeline. Each is a Markdown file with YAML frontmatter.
notes: Agent sequence: brainstormer → planner → researcher → gotcha-checker → reviewers (plan), coder → completeness-checker → reviewers (implement), documenter (apply). Debug/refactor have own paths. Cleanup merged into documenter Step 8b.

name: Skills
description: User-facing skills that trigger FORGE pipelines. Each is a SKILL.md loaded by Claude Code on invocation.
notes: Each skill orchestrates MCP tool calls and agent dispatches. Routing: every agent invocation preceded by forge_get_model_recommendation. Notable: forge:commit skill handles commit+merge (split from forge:approve in wave 3); forge:plan and forge:implement call forge_classify_risk before creating the run.

name: MCP Server
description: The forge-pipeline MCP server exposing 35 forge_* tools for pipeline state, model routing, and external provider calls.
notes: Entry point: mcp/server.js (ESM). Separate mcp/package.json with type:module. Includes router, config-store, usage-store, adapters, dashboard-state, stage-labels, sanitize, model-validation, classify-risk, constraints, patterns, learning. stage-labels now uses stageLabelFromStages() (stages-only, no pipelineMode/currentStep).

name: Run Registry
description: Durable, schema-validated run lifecycle store. Each run has a stable runId, lifecycle state, and on-disk representation.
notes: ESM. Schema enforced by Zod. On-disk: .pipeline/runs/<runId>/run.json + index.json. Also handles worktree creation via createWorktree.js.

name: Hooks
description: Node.js hook scripts wired to Claude Code lifecycle events. Enforce gates, guard bash, track routing, inject context, detect stuck loops.
notes: CJS. 29 scripts declared in hooks/hooks.json across 10 event types: SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PostCompact, Stop, SessionEnd, FileChanged, SubagentStart, SubagentStop. Shared utils in hook-utils.js (resolveProjectDir, stripAnsi, approval tokens, timeout constants, agent allowlist).

name: Observer TUI
description: Read-only terminal-kit split-pane dashboard showing pipeline state: active runs, gates, TODOs, notes, usage.
notes: Auto-launched by hooks/observer-autosplit.js on Windows. Uses terminal-kit (not Ink/React). Tabs: Sessions, TODOs, Notes, Usage. Signal injection via observer-selected.json.

name: Parallel Sessions
description: Git worktree isolation for concurrent pipeline runs. Each pipeline run (plan, implement, debug, refactor) gets its own worktree, branch, and .pipeline/ copy.
notes: Windows-only worker spawning via wt.exe. gate-enforcement.js is worktree-aware. Worktree creation also in packages/forge-core/src/runs/createWorktree.js.

name: Project Scaffolds
description: Bootstrap templates copied by /forge:init. Provides starter CLAUDE.md, docs/, and .pipeline/ for new projects.
notes: Template types: code (JS/TS), code-csharp, power-automate, instructional. Architect customises after init.

name: Dev Tooling
description: Plugin development scripts: tests, version bump, token audit, smoke tests, agent validation.
notes: run-tests.mjs is the regression runner. validate-agents.mjs checks frontmatter. bump-version.mjs updates plugin.json. Observer and dashboard scripts also live here but belong to the observer module.

name: LEAN Risk Gate
description: Classifier that determines whether reviewer dispatch can be skipped in LEAN mode based on handoff risk surface analysis.
notes: Pure function, no I/O. Risk-surface rules cover: bin/, hooks/, mcp/ paths; shell spawn, fs write, auth/crypto, network, schema changes.

name: Commands
description: Remaining slash commands (doctor, hello) that haven't migrated to skills.
notes: Most commands migrated to skills/. Only ping.md remains; doctor.md and hello.md were removed.
